script_text: Mia woke up early on Saturday. She put on her boots and grabbed a basket. She walked to the garden and picked strawberries until the basket was full.. Where did Mia go? The garden. School. The store. The park.

Leo's dog, Biscuit, loved to play fetch. Every afternoon, Leo would throw a tennis ball across the yard. Biscuit always brought it back, wagging his tail.. What did Leo throw? A stick. A tennis ball. A frisbee. A bone.

On the first day of second grade, Emma felt nervous. She didn't know anyone in her new class. At lunch, a girl named Priya sat next to her and said, "Want to share my crackers?". Why was Emma nervous? She was late. She lost her backpack. She didn't know anyone. She forgot her lunch.

Grandpa and Tomas built a birdhouse together. They used wood, nails, and bright blue paint. They hung it on the oak tree in the backyard.. Who helped Tomas build the birdhouse? His mom. His teacher. His friend. His grandpa.

It was raining hard, so the soccer game was canceled. Ava was disappointed. Her dad said, "Let's play board games instead!" They played three rounds of her favorite game.. When was the soccer game canceled? When it was raining hard. When it was sunny. When it was windy. When it was snowing.

The ant worked all summer storing food. The grasshopper played and sang. When winter came, the ant had plenty to eat, but the grasshopper had nothing.. What is the lesson of this story? Singing is fun. It's good to plan ahead. Ants are better than grasshoppers. Winter is cold.

The tortoise and the hare had a race. The hare ran fast but stopped to nap. The tortoise kept going slowly and steadily. The tortoise crossed the finish line first.. What is the moral of this story? Running fast always wins. Napping is healthy. Slow and steady wins the race. Turtles are faster than rabbits.

Rosa wanted the biggest piece of cake. She pushed ahead of everyone in line. Her friends were upset and didn't want to sit with her. Rosa wished she had waited her turn.. What is the lesson of this story? Birthdays are fun. Cake is delicious. Lines are boring. Being patient and fair matters.

A lion was caught in a hunter's net. A tiny mouse chewed through the ropes and set the lion free. The lion had once let the mouse go instead of eating it.. What is the message of this story? Even small friends can be a big help. Hunters are mean. Mice like cheese. Lions are scary.

Ben bragged that he was the best speller in class. During the spelling bee, he didn't study and missed an easy word. His friend Kai, who had practiced every night, won.. What is the central message? Spelling is hard. Hard work beats bragging. Kai got lucky. Ben is a bad speller.

Lily's sandcastle was knocked down by a big wave. She felt sad at first, but then she smiled and said, "I'll build an even bigger one!". How did Lily respond to the challenge? She cried and gave up. She yelled at the wave. She decided to build a bigger one. She went home.

During the talent show, Jake forgot the words to his song. His face turned red. Then he took a deep breath and started over from the beginning.. How did Jake handle forgetting the words? He started crying. He ran off the stage. He asked the teacher for help. He took a deep breath and started over.

When the power went out during the storm, Maya was scared. Her mom lit candles and they told funny stories. Soon Maya was laughing instead of being afraid.. How did Maya's feelings change? She went from scared to laughing. She stayed scared the whole time. She fell asleep. She got angry.

Sam's best friend moved to a different city. Sam was really sad. He asked his mom if they could write letters to each other. Now Sam and his friend are pen pals.. How did Sam respond when his friend moved away? He forgot about his friend. He found a way to stay in touch. He moved too. He was angry at his friend.

Nora's team lost the championship game. Some kids were upset, but Nora high-fived every player and said, "We played our best. I'm proud of us.". What does Nora's response tell us about her? She didn't care about winning. She was secretly angry. She is a good sport. She doesn't like soccer.

The rain came down, drip, drip, drop. It wouldn't quit, it wouldn't stop!. What do the words drip, drip, drop help you imagine? A sunny day. Wind blowing. A loud thunderstorm. The sound of rain falling.

Swoosh went the broom across the floor. Swish, swish, swoosh — it swept some more!. The words swoosh and swish help the reader — Hear the sound of sweeping. Learn how to clean. See the color of the broom. Know what time it is.
Silly Sally sat on a seal, singing songs by the sea.. What makes this sentence fun to say? It has lots of periods. Many words start with the same sound. It rhymes at the end. It has big words.
The leaves danced in the wind, twirling and spinning to the ground.. The word danced makes the leaves seem — Wet and cold. Heavy and slow. Lively and playful. Scary and dark.

Tick-tock, tick-tock, the clock on the wall. Tick-tock, tick-tock, it counts for us all.. Why does the author repeat tick-tock? To make it scary. Because they forgot what to write. To make the poem longer. To copy the sound and rhythm of a clock.

Once upon a time, a girl named Zara found a magic key. She used it to open a secret door in her attic. Behind the door was a land of talking animals. After many adventures, Zara returned home and locked the door.. Which part is the beginning of the story? Zara found a magic key. She locked the door. The land of talking animals. Zara returned home.
In a story, what does the ending usually do? Describe the setting. Wrap up the story and solve the problem. Introduce new characters. Start a new problem.

Devin wanted to win the art contest. He painted every day after school. His painting of a sunset won first place!. What is the problem at the beginning? Devin went to school. Devin won a prize. Devin wanted to win the contest. Devin painted a sunset.
Which part of a story introduces the characters and setting? The title. The ending. The middle. The beginning.

Mia lost her puppy in the park. She looked everywhere. Finally, she heard barking behind the slide and found her puppy playing with another dog.. How does the ending conclude the action? Mia finds her puppy. Mia loses her puppy. Mia looks everywhere. Mia goes to the park.

Ana thought the roller coaster was amazing and wanted to ride again. Her brother Carlos shook his head and said, "No way, that was too scary!". How are Ana's and Carlos's feelings different? Both were scared. Ana loved it but Carlos was scared. Carlos loved it but Ana didn't. Both wanted to ride again.

Mom said, "We should get a cat." Dad said, "I think a dog would be better." Jayden said, "Can we get both?". Who has a different point of view from Mom? Only Dad. Nobody. Both Dad and Jayden. Only Jayden.

The farmer was happy when it rained because his crops needed water. The children were sad because they couldn't play outside.. Why do the farmer and the children feel differently about the rain? The farmer likes being wet. They live in different places. The children don't like water. Rain helps the farmer's crops but stops the children's play.

Ella wanted to stay up late to watch a movie. Her mom said it was a school night and bedtime was 8 o'clock.. How is Ella's point of view different from her mom's? Ella wants to stay up but mom wants her to go to bed. Ella wants to sleep but mom says no. They both agree on bedtime. Mom wants to watch the movie too.

In the story, the Big Bad Wolf said, "I just wanted to borrow some sugar!" The Three Little Pigs said, "He tried to blow our houses down!". How are the points of view different? They agree on what happened. The wolf says he was friendly but the pigs say he was dangerous. The pigs like the wolf. The wolf admits he was bad.

If a story says Tomas felt happy and the picture shows him with a big smile and jumping, what do the words AND picture together tell you? Tomas is pretending. Tomas is a little happy. Tomas is very happy. Tomas is sick.

A story's words say: "It was a dark night." The picture shows rain, lightning, and bent trees. What can you tell about the setting? It's snowing. It's a calm evening. It's a sunny morning. There's a big storm.
Why are illustrations important in a story? They help you understand the characters and setting better. They replace the words. They are only for decoration. They make the book heavier.

A story says "Maya walked into the room." The picture shows Maya with wide eyes, an open mouth, and a room full of balloons and presents. What is probably happening? Maya is sad. Maya is at a surprise party. Maya is going to bed. Maya is at school.

The text says: "The old man sat on the bench." The picture shows him feeding pigeons with a gentle smile and a cane beside him. What do you learn from the picture that the words don't tell you? He's an old man. He's outside. He's feeding pigeons and seems kind. He's sitting on a bench.

Two versions of Cinderella both have a girl who goes to a ball. In one version, a fairy godmother helps her. In the other, magic fish help her. What is the SAME in both? The magic fish. The prince's name. The fairy godmother. A girl goes to a ball.
When you compare two versions of the same story, you look for things that are — Both the same AND different. Only different. Only the same. Not in either story.

In an American version of a folktale, the hero is a cowboy. In a Japanese version, the hero is a samurai. Both heroes save their village. What is DIFFERENT? The heroes are brave. The type of hero. Both save a village. The stories have a happy ending.

Story A: A boy finds a golden egg laid by a goose. Story B: A girl finds a golden seed that grows into a money tree. How are these stories similar? Both are about boys. Both have a goose. Both characters find something magical and valuable. Both have a tree.

Why might the same story be told differently in different cultures? People in other places don't like stories. The authors copied each other. One version is right and the other is wrong. Each culture adds its own setting, characters, and traditions.

The wind howled through the trees as Sophie clutched her flashlight. She had promised to walk to the mailbox alone, and she wasn't going to break her promise. With each step, the shadows seemed to dance around her.. How is Sophie feeling? Brave but a little scared. Bored and tired. Angry at someone. Excited and happy.

Jackson's painting looked nothing like the sunset he was trying to copy. He almost crumpled it up, but then his art teacher said, "Art doesn't have to be perfect — it just has to be yours.". What did Jackson learn? Art should be a perfect copy. His own style matters. His teacher is mean. He should stop painting.

Every day, the old lighthouse keeper climbed 137 steps to light the lamp. Ships depend on me, he would say. Even in the worst storms, the light always shone.. What word BEST describes the lighthouse keeper? Lazy. Silly. Dedicated. Confused.

The squirrel buried an acorn in the fall. By spring, a tiny green sprout poked through the soil. Years later, it became the tallest oak in the forest.. What is this story mainly about? Forests have lots of trees. Spring is the best season. Squirrels are forgetful. Small things can grow into something big.

"I'll never make friends here," whispered Amir on his first day at the new school. By the end of the week, he had joined the soccer team and made three new friends.. What happened that Amir did NOT expect? He made friends quickly. He went to a new school. He felt nervous. He played soccer.

Honeybees live in hives. A hive can have up to 60,000 bees! The queen bee lays all the eggs, and worker bees collect nectar from flowers.. What does the queen bee do? Guards the hive. Lays all the eggs. Collects nectar. Builds the hive.

Firefighters wear special gear to stay safe. Their helmets protect their heads. Their thick coats keep them safe from fire and heat.. Why do firefighters wear thick coats? Because they like heavy clothes. To look cool. To keep safe from fire and heat. To stay warm in winter.
George Washington Carver was a scientist who studied peanuts. He found over 300 uses for peanuts, including making soap, ink, and dye.. How many uses did Carver find for peanuts? 3,000. 30. 100. 300.

Sea turtles travel thousands of miles through the ocean. They return to the same beach where they were born to lay their eggs.. Where do sea turtles lay their eggs? On the beach where they were born. In the deep ocean. On any beach. In a nest in a tree.

In 1969, astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walked on the Moon for the first time. Millions of people watched on television.. When did astronauts first walk on the Moon? 1979. 1969. 1959. 1989.

Dogs make great pets. They are loyal and friendly. Many dogs can be trained to do tricks. Some dogs even help people who are blind.. What is the main topic? How to train pets. Cats. Why dogs make great pets. Animals at the zoo.

Paragraph 1: Apples come in many colors — red, green, and yellow. Paragraph 2: Apples are good for you because they have vitamins and fiber.. What is Paragraph 2 mostly about? The colors of apples. How to grow apples. Apple pie recipes. Why apples are healthy.

A passage has three paragraphs. One is about where penguins live. One is about what they eat. One is about how they raise their babies. What is the MAIN topic of the whole passage? All about penguins. Penguin food. Antarctic weather. Baby animals.

Water can be a solid, a liquid, or a gas. Ice is solid water. Steam is water as a gas. The water you drink is a liquid.. What is the main topic? How to make steam. The three forms of water. Ice is cold. Drinking water.
How can you find the main topic of a passage with many paragraphs? Count the sentences. Read only the first word. Think about what ALL the paragraphs are about together. Look at the pictures only.

First, the caterpillar eats lots of leaves. Then, it makes a chrysalis. Finally, it comes out as a butterfly.. What happens AFTER the caterpillar makes a chrysalis? It makes another chrysalis. It eats more leaves. It goes to sleep forever. It becomes a butterfly.

The sun heats the ocean water. The water evaporates into the air. Clouds form. Then rain falls back to the ground.. What causes clouds to form? Water evaporating into the air. The moon. Rain falling. Wind blowing.

In 1903, the Wright Brothers flew the first airplane. Their flight lasted only 12 seconds. Today, planes can fly across the whole world.. How are the events connected? Modern planes were invented first. The Wright Brothers' invention led to modern airplanes. They are not related. The Wright Brothers flew across the world.

To make a peanut butter sandwich: First, get two slices of bread. Next, spread peanut butter on one slice. Then, put the other slice on top.. What do you do right AFTER spreading peanut butter? Get the bread. Eat the sandwich. Put the other slice on top. Open the jar.

When bees visit flowers, pollen sticks to their legs. They carry the pollen to other flowers. This helps new plants grow.. How does pollen getting on bees connect to new plants growing? Plants grow on their own. Pollen makes bees fly faster. Bees eat the plants. Bees carry pollen to other flowers, which helps plants grow.

First, the ocean water near the shore gets warm from the sun. Then, the warm water evaporates and rises into the air. Finally, it forms clouds that bring rain.. What happens AFTER the water evaporates? It forms clouds. The sun goes down. Fish swim away. The shore gets bigger.

Sea otters eat sea urchins. When there are fewer sea otters, the number of sea urchins grows. As a result, the sea urchins eat too much kelp, and the kelp forests disappear.. What causes the kelp forests to disappear? Too many sea otters. Too many sea urchins eating the kelp. Strong ocean waves. Cold water.

Clownfish live inside sea anemones. The anemone's stinging tentacles protect the clownfish from predators. In return, the clownfish cleans the anemone and brings it food.. How are the clownfish and anemone connected? They fight each other. They live far apart. They help each other. One eats the other.

To make sea salt, workers first pump ocean water into shallow pools. Next, the sun heats the water until it evaporates. Then, only the salt is left behind. Finally, workers collect the salt.. What is the SECOND step in making sea salt? Workers collect the salt. The salt is left behind. Workers pump ocean water. The sun heats the water.

Plastic bags look like jellyfish in the water. Because of this, sea turtles sometimes eat the plastic by mistake. This can make them very sick.. Why do sea turtles eat plastic bags? The bags look like jellyfish. They are hungry for plastic. Other turtles told them to. They like the taste.

Since the ocean near the shore is shallow, sunlight can reach the bottom. This allows plants to grow on the ocean floor. Many small fish eat those plants.. What allows plants to grow on the ocean floor? Cold water. Sunlight reaching the bottom. Deep water. Strong currents.
Nocturnal animals are active at night. Owls, bats, and raccoons are nocturnal.. What does nocturnal mean? Very fast. Living in water. Active at night. Active during the day.
A habitat is the natural home of an animal or plant. A frog's habitat is near ponds and streams.. What is a habitat? A type of food. A kind of weather. A baby animal. A natural home.

Mammals are warm-blooded animals that have fur or hair. Dogs, cats, and humans are all mammals.. Based on the text, what makes an animal a mammal? It is warm-blooded with fur or hair. It can fly. It lays eggs. It lives in water.
The ancient Egyptians built enormous pyramids. These huge stone structures still stand today.. What does enormous mean in this sentence? Very colorful. Very huge. Very old. Very small.

Plants need nutrients from the soil to grow. Nutrients are like food for plants — they give plants energy and help them stay healthy.. What are nutrients? A type of soil. Sunlight. Food-like substances that help plants grow. Water.
You want to find the meaning of the word ecosystem in a science book. Where should you look? The title page. The table of contents. The index. The glossary.
What is the purpose of a heading in a nonfiction text? To tell you what that section is about. To end the chapter. To list the author's name. To decorate the page.
You want to know which page has information about dolphins. Which text feature should you use? Bold print. The index. The glossary. A caption.
In a nonfiction book, some words are in bold print. Why? To make the page look pretty. Because the author likes those words. To show important or new vocabulary words. Because they are misspelled.
A small sentence under a photograph that explains what's in the picture is called a — Title. Heading. Paragraph. Caption.

Brush your teeth twice a day. Use toothpaste with fluoride. Visit the dentist every six months.. What is the author's purpose? To explain how to take care of your teeth. To tell a funny story. To describe a toothbrush. To sell toothpaste.

The blue whale is the largest animal ever to live on Earth. It can be over 100 feet long and weigh as much as 200 tons.. The author wrote this to — Tell a story about a whale. Inform you about blue whales. Convince you to visit the ocean. Make you laugh.

Everyone should recycle! When we recycle, we save trees and keep trash out of the ocean. Please recycle your cans, bottles, and paper.. The author's main purpose is to — Describe what recycling looks like. Teach you how paper is made. Persuade you to recycle. Entertain you with a story.
A book that teaches you about different types of clouds was written to — Persuade. Scare. Entertain. Inform.

Butterflies have four stages in their life: egg, caterpillar, chrysalis, and butterfly. Each stage is an important part of their life cycle.. What question is the author trying to answer? What are the stages of a butterfly's life?. Why are butterflies colorful?. Where do butterflies live?. How fast can butterflies fly?.

A science book has a paragraph about the parts of a plant. Next to it is a diagram with labels pointing to the roots, stem, leaves, and flower. How does the diagram help? It makes the page look nice. It shows you exactly where each plant part is. It replaces the paragraph. It shows a different topic.

A text about weather says: "A thermometer measures temperature." Below it is a picture of a thermometer showing 32°F. What does the picture add? It replaces the need to read. Nothing new. It shows what a thermometer looks like and how to read it. It tells you about rain.
Why would an author include a map in a book about animals around the world? To make the book longer. To show what animals eat. To teach you to draw. To show where different animals live.

A book about healthy eating includes a picture of a food plate divided into sections: fruits, vegetables, grains, protein, and dairy. This image helps the reader — See how much of each food group to eat. Learn to draw plates. Know what to buy at the store. Cook a meal.
Which type of image would BEST help explain how a bicycle works? A photo of someone walking. A labeled diagram showing bicycle parts. A painting of a bicycle race. A photograph of a sunset.

Recess is important for kids. It gives them time to exercise. It also helps them focus better in class.. What reason does the author give for why recess helps in class? Kids like to play. It makes school longer. It helps them focus better. Teachers need a break.
Everyone should drink more water. Water keeps your body healthy. It helps your brain work better. It also gives you energy.. How many reasons does the author give for drinking water? 2. 4. 1. 3.

Helmets should be worn when riding a bike. They protect your head if you fall. Studies show helmets reduce head injuries by 85%.. What supports the author's point that helmets are important? They reduce head injuries by 85%. They come in many colors. Everyone has a bike. Helmets look cool.
When an author writes because in a sentence, what usually follows? The end of the paragraph. A reason that supports their point. A question. A new topic.

Schools should have gardens. Gardens teach kids about science. They also help kids eat healthier because kids are more likely to eat vegetables they grew themselves.. Which reason explains HOW gardens help kids eat healthier? Gardens are pretty. Schools have space for gardens. Kids eat vegetables they grew themselves. Gardens teach science.

Text A says dogs are great pets because they are loyal. Text B says dogs are great pets because they can be trained. What do BOTH texts agree on? Dogs are better than cats. Dogs can be trained. Dogs are loyal. Dogs are great pets.

Text A about the ocean focuses on ocean animals. Text B about the ocean focuses on pollution in the ocean. How are these texts different? They focus on different aspects of the same topic. Text A is fiction. They are about different topics. They say the same things.

Text A: "Bats use sound to find food in the dark." Text B: "Bats eat insects and help farmers by keeping pests away.". What does Text B tell you that Text A does not? Bats are mammals. Bats help farmers. Bats use sound. Bats fly at night.

When comparing two texts on the same topic, what should you look for? Which text has more pictures. Which text was written first. What information is the same and what is different. Which text is longer.

Text A says: "Exercise keeps your heart healthy." Text B says: "Exercise helps you sleep better and makes your bones strong.". Which benefit is mentioned in Text A but NOT Text B? Stronger bones. More energy. Better sleep. A healthy heart.

Octopuses are amazing creatures. They have eight arms and three hearts. They can change color to blend in with their surroundings. Some octopuses can even open jars!. According to the text, how many hearts does an octopus have? Three. Eight. One. Two.

The Great Wall of China was built over many centuries to protect China from invaders. It stretches over 13,000 miles, making it the longest structure ever built by humans.. Why was the Great Wall built? To divide cities. To protect from invaders. For decoration. For tourists to visit.

Recycling one aluminum can saves enough energy to power a TV for three hours. Americans throw away enough cans each year to build more than 20 Eiffel Towers.. What is the author trying to show with these facts? Americans watch too much TV. The Eiffel Tower is made of cans. Recycling cans saves a lot of energy. TVs use too much power.

Polar bears have black skin under their white fur. The white fur helps them hide in the snow. Their large paws work like snowshoes, helping them walk on ice.. What helps polar bears walk on ice? Their sharp teeth. Their black skin. Their white fur. Their large paws.

Volcanoes form when hot melted rock called magma pushes up through the Earth's surface. When magma comes out, it's called lava. Over time, the lava cools and builds up to form the volcano's cone shape.. What is magma called after it comes out of the volcano? Lava. Rock. Ash. Steam.
Which word has a long vowel sound? Cap. Cake. Cat. Can.
What is the root word in playing? Paying. Ing. Play. Lay.
Which word has the prefix un-? Uncle. Until. Under. Unhappy.
How many syllables are in the word pumpkin? 2. 4. 1. 3.
Which word is spelled correctly? Becaws. Because. Becuz. Becuase.
Which word has a SHORT a sound? Rain. Bake. Map. Day.
Which word has a LONG o sound? Hot. Hop. Lot. Home.
The word bed has a — Short 'e' sound. Long 'a' sound. Long 'e' sound. Short 'i' sound.
Which pair shows a short vowel word and its long vowel partner? Dog → dig. Pin → pine. Run → ran. Cat → cut.
Which word does NOT have a long vowel sound? Cube. Bike. Milk. Mule.
In the word boat, what sound does the vowel team oa make? Short 'a'. Long 'a'. Short 'o'. Long 'o'.
Which word has the ee vowel team? Tree. Red. Bed. Ten.
The vowel team ai in rain makes the same sound as ay in — Ant. Play. About. All.
Which word has the same vowel sound as seat? Set. Fit. Feet. Sat.
Which vowel team is in the word loud? Oi. Oo. Oa. Ou.
Break the word cupcake into two syllables: Cup-cake. Cu-pcake. C-upcake. Cupc-ake.
The word robot has two syllables. How do you break it apart? Rob-ot. Ro-bot. Robo-t. R-obot.
Which two-syllable word has a long i sound? Mitten. Kitten. Spider. Rabbit.
How many syllables are in the word music? 1. 4. 3. 2.
Break open into syllables: O-pen. Open. Ope-n. Op-en.
The suffix -ful in hopeful means — Before. Full of. Not. Without.
Which word has a suffix? Inside. Undo. Slowly. Replay.
Which word does NOT follow regular phonics rules? Dog. Run. Cat. Said.
The word have is tricky because — It ends with 'e' but the 'a' is short. The 'a' doesn't make the expected sound. It has too many letters. It has a silent 'h'.
Which word has a silent letter? Cup. Know. Stop. Lamp.
In the word love, the o makes the sound of — Long 'o' (like 'bone'). Long 'u' (like 'cute'). Short 'u' (like 'cup'). Short 'o' (like 'hot').
Which of these words is a tricky sight word that doesn't follow phonics rules? Pot. Big. Map. The.
Which word is spelled correctly? People. Peopel. Peeple. Peple.
Choose the correct spelling: Frend. Friend. Freind. Frind.
Which sentence uses their correctly? Their going to the park.. Their is a dog outside.. They put their books away.. I went their yesterday..
Choose the correct spelling of the word that means one more time: Agane. Agin. Agen. Again.
Which word is an irregularly spelled word you need to memorize? Because. Help. Stop. Jump.
What does it mean to read fluently? Reading silently. Reading smoothly with expression. Reading very fast. Reading every word perfectly.
When you see an exclamation mark (!) at the end of a sentence, you should read it with — A quiet voice. A question in your voice. Excitement or strong feeling. No change in voice.
If you don't understand a sentence after reading it, what should you do? Ask someone to read the whole book. Close the book. Skip it and keep going. Read it again more carefully.
"Stop!" yelled the crossing guard.. How should you read the word Stop? In a loud, firm voice. Very slowly. In a silly voice. In a whisper.
When a good reader comes to a word they don't know, they — Stop reading the book. Use clues from the sentence to figure it out. Skip the whole page. Guess randomly.

The farmer planted seeds in the spring. He watered them every day. By summer, his garden was full of tomatoes, peppers, and corn.. What grew in the farmer's garden? Apples. Wheat. Tomatoes, peppers, and corn. Flowers.
Before you start reading, it helps to ask yourself — Who wrote this book?. How many pages is this?. What color is the cover?. What do I want to learn from this?.

Dolphins are mammals, not fish. They breathe air and feed milk to their babies. Dolphins are very smart and can learn tricks.. What makes dolphins different from fish? They breathe air and feed milk to babies. They have tails. They swim. They live in the ocean.

Ben's favorite book is about space. He reads it every week because he wants to be an astronaut someday.. Why does Ben read the space book so often? He has to for school. He wants to be an astronaut. It's the only book he has. His mom makes him.

Earthworms help gardens by making tunnels in the soil. The tunnels let air and water reach plant roots.. How do earthworm tunnels help plants? They make the garden look nice. They keep bugs away. They let air and water reach the roots. They make the soil warmer.
How should you read this sentence? "I can't believe we won the game!" In a whisper. Slowly and sadly. With no expression. With excitement and energy.
When you read a question aloud, your voice should — Go up at the end. Stay the same. Get very loud. Go down at the end.
A character in a story whispers, "Shhh, the baby is sleeping." How should you read this line? In a loud voice. Softly and quietly. With an angry tone. Very fast.

What does reading at an appropriate rate mean? Reading so slowly that it's hard to follow. Skipping every other word. Reading at a comfortable speed — not too fast, not too slow. Reading as fast as possible.
How should you read this line from a character who is scared? "W-what was that noise?" Very fast and cheerful. Happily and loud. In a bored voice. With a shaky, nervous voice.

You read: "The dog buried a bone in the yard." But you accidentally read bone as "bun." What should you do? Go back and reread because 'bun' doesn't make sense. Ask someone to read for you. Keep reading. Stop reading the book.
What is a good strategy when a sentence doesn't make sense? Skip the whole page. Reread the sentence and try the hard word again. Read faster. Close the book.
"The enormous elephant was the biggest animal at the zoo.". The word enormous means — Gray. Slow. Very large. Tiny.
"The frigid wind made everyone shiver and bundle up in their coats.". The word frigid means — Gentle. Warm. Strong. Very cold.
The word preview means — To view before. To not view. To view after. To view again.
If teach means to help someone learn, what does a teacher do? Drives a bus. Helps people learn. Builds things. Cooks food.
The root word help is in all of these words EXCEPT — Helper. Helpless. Helmet. Helpful.
What does sunflower mean? A type of sunshine. A flower that blocks the sun. The sun made of flowers. A flower that faces the sun.
The word raincoat is made up of which two words? Rain + coat. Rai + ncoat. Rainco + at. Ra + incoat.
What is a bookshelf? A type of book. A shelf for holding books. A book about shelves. A shelf made of books.

If light means something that shines and house means a building, what is a lighthouse? A light inside a house. A house that is light-colored. A building with a bright light to guide ships. A house made of light.
Which word is a compound word? Garden. Sister. Purple. Butterfly.
Where would you find the meaning of a word in a nonfiction book? The glossary. The title page. The back cover. The table of contents.
Words in a dictionary are organized — By color. In alphabetical order. By topic. By length.
Which word would come FIRST in a dictionary? Cat. Dog. Ant. Bird.
A glossary is different from a dictionary because — It's at the front of the book. It has more words. It has pictures. It only includes words used in that specific book.
You want to look up penguin in the dictionary. Which guide words tell you it's on that page? Peach — person. Phone — picture. Play — plum. Paper — party.
Which word means almost the same as big? Tiny. Large. Bright. Fast.
Which word is the OPPOSITE of hot? Sunny. Spicy. Cold. Warm.
When would you use the word whisper? At a loud concert. When calling someone far away. While cheering at a game. In a library when you need to be quiet.
Which word means the same as glad? Happy. Scared. Angry. Sad.
Which of these foods could be described as crunchy? Soup. Carrots. Ice cream. Yogurt.
Which action is courageous? Watching TV. Sleeping in your bed. Standing up to a bully. Eating lunch.
When might you feel frustrated? When you open a birthday present. When you eat your favorite food. When you play with your best friend. When you can't solve a puzzle after many tries.
Which place would you describe as peaceful? A quiet garden with birds singing. A crowded mall. A construction site. A loud gym class.
Which animal would you describe as swift? A snail. A cheetah. A sloth. A turtle.
Which word means being MORE than just warm? Cold. Cool. Hot. Chilly.
Which word shows a BIGGER size than big? Medium. Tiny. Small. Gigantic.
Which word means walking SLOWLY and tiredly? Trudge. March. Sprint. Skip.
Which sentence uses an adjective to describe the dog? Dogs are pets. The fluffy dog ran.. The dog ran. The dog ran quickly..
Which word is an adverb in this sentence?. "The girl sang beautifully." Sang. Girl. Beautifully. The.
Which sentence uses an adverb? She ate a tasty cookie.. The cat is orange.. The tall tree fell.. He ran quickly to the bus..
Choose the MOST descriptive sentence: I hungrily ate a warm, cheesy sandwich.. I ate something good.. I ate a sandwich.. I ate food.
Pick the right word: The team ___ to the field. run. runs. running. ran to.
Which is the past tense of fly? flied. flyed. flew. flying.
Which sentence is correct? I happy today.. Me am happy.. I is happy.. I am happy today..
What is a collective noun? a word for a group. a long sentence. a big letter. a verb.
Past tense of eat? eated. ate. eats. eating.
Which shows the dog owns the bone? the dogs bone. the dog bone. the dog's bone. the dogs' bone.
What does can't mean? could not. will not. cannot yet. can not.
Which sentence uses commas correctly? I like apples, bananas, and grapes.. I like apples bananas and grapes.. I, like apples, bananas, and, grapes.. I like, apples, bananas, and grapes..
Let's is short for: let is. let us. let as. let it.
Where do you put a comma in a list? at the start. at the end. between each item. never.
If you are writing to a teacher, which sounds best? Thanks a bunch!. Thx a lot.. Yo thanks.. Thank you for your help..
Which word is most formal? assist. help. lend a hand. back up.
Which sentence sounds too casual for a school essay? It is very interesting.. Yeah, it's super cool.. It was a fine experience.. I enjoyed it..
Which word is a stronger way to say big? kinda big. big-ish. enormous. medium.
Why does word choice matter? to look smart. to be funny. to use long words. words carry different feelings.
